channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Not to Alarm Anyone, but Flesh-Eating Screwworms Have Entered the US
item.description: The USDA this week confirmed the first known infection of the carnivorous fly larva, which feast on the flesh of living mammals, after the United States eradicated the nightmare bugs in the 1960s.

item.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
item.description: With just two weeks to go, StrictlyVC Los Angeles is quickly approaching. On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus in El Segundo. Investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversations exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure your spot here. For […]...

item.title: AIs like ChatGPT fall apart in classic 'Stroop' psychological test — and that could stand in the way of achieving artificial general intelligence
item.description: New research is causing quite some controversy on Reddit — but it makes some very interesting points.

item.title: Best Buy slashes up to $400 off Apple tech in a limited-time sale — get AirPods, MacBooks, iPads and Apple Watches from $99.99
item.description: Best Buy has launched a very limited-time Apple shopping event this week, so I've searched through the sale and picked out the 12 best deals on AirPods, MacBooks, iPads, Apple Watches, and more.

item.title: Georgie & Mandy's First Marriage season 3: release date predictions, cast, plot, and everything we know about the Young Sheldon spinoff's return to CBS and Paramount+
item.description: Season 2 has only just ended, but all eyes are on new episodes — here's what we know about Georgie & Mandy's First Marriage season 3's return to CBS and Paramount+.

item.title: Startup Battlefield is returning to Australia — here’s what happened the last time we came to Sydney
item.description: On August 19, Startup Battlefield is returning to Sydney in partnership with Stripe, one of the world's most iconic technology companies. We're taking over Stripe Tour Sydney for a night that the Australian startup ecosystem won't forget.

item.title: The Instagram Plus subscription has officially launched
item.description: Paying users will have tools for reaching either wider or more-specific audiences.

item.title: Wired found code for an unreleased facial recognition feature in Meta's AI app
item.description: Meta was previously reported to be exploring facial recognition for its smart glasses.

item.title: Anthropic says 80% of its new production code is now authored by Claude — how your enterprise can keep up
item.description: Anthropic co-founder and CEO Dario Amodei said it was coming, but it still feels like a milestone: More than 80% of the code merged into Anthropic’s production codebase in May wasn't authored by humans, but by its own AI model, Claude, according to a new report shared by the record-breaking AI startup today.This transformation has triggered an 8x increase in the volume of code shipped per engineer per quarter compared to the company’s 2021–2025 baseline, which the company notes means even more code someone or something must review.For enterprise technical leaders, this is no longer a localized...

item.title: ChatGPT's memory is getting better, especially if you're on the free tier
item.description: OpenAI has significantly improved the chatbot's "dreaming" architecture.

item.title: Many US tech firms are turning to China's DeepSeek as the bill for homegrown AI bites – American AI companies could learn a thing or two
item.description: American models are simply too expensive – US firms are now turning to DeepSeek for cheaper costs and portability.

item.title: I taught Gemini my learning style to understand quantum physics 10x faster with custom analogies and daily quizzes
item.description: Find out how Google Gemini can adapt to your learning style with personalized analogies, simplified explanations, and daily quizzes to make any topic easier to understand.

item.title: OpenAI’s Codex helps discover HTTP/2 Bomb DoS attack that can nuke over 30GB of RAM within seconds, knocking web servers offline before they can react
item.description: A new attack technique affects HTTP/2 configurations of major web servers, but some have released patches already.

item.title: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
item.description: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The Cut and Wired, went viral earlier this year with a mermaid-themed cyberdeck she made inside an old purse. She has since […]

item.title: With GTA 6 Online, Rockstar has a chance to free players from its greatest vehicular mistake
item.description: For several years now, GTA Online players have been watching the skies with fear. It’s time for that to end.

item.title: I'm done with multitasking on a single PC, and I'm tired of waiting for Valve — so I built a custom Steam Machine, and here's what it has changed for me
item.description: PC gaming and multitasking can't get any easier than a dual PC setup, especially with the help of a capture card, and it's the best choice I've made in years.

item.title: Images truquées | Une députée britannique poursuit xAI
item.description: Une députée britannique a annoncé jeudi qu’elle poursuivait xAI, une entreprise d’Elon Musk, pour atteinte à la vie privée, affirmant que des images truquées d’elle avaient été créées à l’aide de l’agent conversationnel Grok.

item.title: 5 things to expect at WWDC 2026 — from Siri 2.0 to Tim Cook's Apple farewell
item.description: Apple's next developer conference kicks off on June 8 — here are 5 announcements we're expecting Tim Cook to make in his final WWDC keynote.
